seoTitle: Cardup vs Carey Park — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Cardup and Carey Park. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Cardup vs Carey Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cardup and Carey Park.
heading: Cardup vs Carey Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cardup, Western Australia and Carey Park, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Carey Park has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Cardup (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cardup or Carey Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cardup has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Carey Park scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cardup scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. Carey Park scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cardup or Carey Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Carey Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
